metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete applies_when tag value(s) for the new foundation/enhancement move (e.g. the exact criterion IDs like 'F1' or 'P8' that represent sizing/coverage findings), since no existing plan-review move-gating tag set exists to 'match'.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'the applies_when vocabulary is the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry', but reading passes.py:737-741 and workflow_ops.py:303-304 confirms ALL existing plan-review moves declare no applies_when (always-applicable). There is no pre-existing applies_when tag set for plan-review moves — so the plan's scoping instruction ('matching how existing moves are offered') references a vocabulary that does not exist, leaving the implementer without a concrete tag to place in applies_when for the new foundation/enhancement move.
findings.location: What section: 'Add foundation/enhancement move... applies_when field scoping it to sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'matching how existing moves are offered' with the concrete criterion IDs that trigger this move, e.g. `"applies_when": ["F1", "P8"]` (F1 = AC quality / right_sized; P8 = reviewability/size), or explicitly state 'always-applicable' if no scoping is intended.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact applies_when tag value(s) for the new foundation/enhancement move (e.g. a criteria id like 'S1' or a new tag constant), and confirm that value will appear in plan-review's active_triggers when sizing/coverage findings survive.
findings.finding: The plan states the new move's applies_when scopes to 'sizing/coverage findings' using 'the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry', but plan-review's active_triggers are arbitrary criteria strings (e.g. rubric ids like 'F1', 'G1G2') derived from surviving findings — there is no closed OVERLAY_IDS vocabulary as in code_review. No concrete tag value is specified, making the applies_when field unenforceably underspecified.
findings.location: What section ('it CARRIES an applies_when field scoping it to sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered); the applies_when vocabulary is the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact criteria IDs for the foundation/enhancement move's `applies_when` field (e.g. the plan-review sizing/decomposition criterion ID such as 'G5'), replacing the conceptual label 'sizing/coverage findings'; also correct the erroneous parenthetical — existing plan-review moves are always-applicable (no applies_when), not a model for this move's scoping.
findings.finding: The plan specifies applies_when for the new foundation/enhancement move using a conceptual label ('sizing/coverage findings') but does not name the actual criteria IDs that constitute the tag set. In plan-review, active_triggers are populated from surviving findings' criteria[] fields (passes.py:777: `triggers = {c for f in surviving for c in f.get('criteria', []) or []}`), so applies_when values must be real criteria IDs (e.g. 'G5', 'F1'). An applies_when list populated with semantic labels that never match any criteria ID makes the move permanently dead (applicable_moves() returns empty intersection). Additionally, the plan's parenthetical justification — 'matching how existing moves are offered' — is factually wrong: all existing plan-review moves have no applies_when and are always-applicable.
findings.location: What section — 'Add foundation/enhancement move … the applies_when vocabulary is the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered)' with the actual criteria IDs from criteria_routing.json that cover sizing and coverage concerns (e.g. applies_when: ["G5", "E1"]) and note that this is the FIRST plan-review move with applies_when, departing from the always-applicable default.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `applies_when` tag vocabulary for the new foundation/enhancement move: document whether the values will be criterion IDs (e.g., `["G5"]` for sizing/decomposition findings) or a new named-tag layer, and confirm that at least one surviving finding's `criteria` list will intersect the chosen tags so the move is not dead.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new foundation/enhancement move will carry an `applies_when` scoping 'using the existing move-gating tag set' targeting 'sizing/coverage findings,' but the actual `active_triggers` fed to `applicable_moves()` in plan-review are criterion IDs (e.g., 'G5', 'F1') collected from surviving findings' `criteria` field — not named domain tags like 'sizing' or 'coverage'. Using those labels as `applies_when` values would produce a dead move that never fires.
findings.location: What section: 'the applies_when vocabulary is the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry'; passes.py:777 / workflow_ops.py:306
findings.suggested_fix: Use criterion IDs directly as the `applies_when` values, e.g., `"applies_when": ["G5"]` to scope the move to decomposition/sizing findings, and/or `"applies_when": ["E1"]` for coverage findings. Add a comment mirroring the code_review pattern (moves.py:58-61) explaining the closed vocabulary. Update the AC to name the exact tag strings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `applies_when` tag value(s) for the new foundation/enhancement move — either enumerate the criterion id(s) (e.g. `['F1', 'right_sized']`) or define a named plan-review tag vocabulary analogous to code-review's `{OVERLAY_IDS ∪ 'always'}` and declare which tags map to 'sizing/coverage findings'.
findings.finding: The `applies_when` tag vocabulary for the new foundation/enhancement move is undefined. The plan says it 'CARRIES an applies_when field scoping it to sizing/coverage findings (matching how existing moves are offered)' and calls this 'the existing move-gating tag set in load_move_registry.' But grep confirms: no existing plan-review move in MOVE_REGISTRY uses `applies_when` at all — the tag set is empty. The plan-review `active_triggers` is built from criterion ids (e.g. 'F1', 'right_sized', 'T8') carried by surviving findings, not from a named closed vocabulary. An executor cannot determine which specific tag(s) to put in `applies_when` for the new move to fire correctly on sizing/coverage findings.
findings.location: What section / 'foundation/enhancement move added to MOVE_REGISTRY … with its verbatim locked template + an applies_when scoping' / AC line 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note to the What section: 'applies_when: ["F1", "right_sized"]` — the two criterion ids that encode a scope/sizing finding in plan-review (F1/right_sized = AC coherence; use ["always"] if broad applicability is acceptable and scoping is aspirational).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py` to the list of consumers to keep green (or explicitly note that its always-applicable assertion must be updated to exclude the new applies_when-scoped move).
findings.finding: tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py:479-481 iterates passes.MOVE_REGISTRY and asserts every move passes move_applies(move, active_triggers=[]) — i.e., no move has applies_when set. Adding the new foundation/enhancement move WITH an applies_when field will cause this test to fail, but it is not listed among the consumers the plan requires to stay green.
findings.location: What section / AC3 ('All MOVE_REGISTRY consumers still pass'); tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py:479-481
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, append `tests/unit/test_review_kernel.py` to 'Consumers to keep green'; in AC3 add it to the proof list. The test's loop may need a guard: `if not move.get('applies_when'): assert kcoach.move_applies(move, active_triggers=[])`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the exact move id that AC4's coach-mapping test should assert is selected (e.g. 'discovered_from_follow_up' or whatever the registry key will be) so the proving command is concrete.
findings.finding: AC4's proving command references a 'follow-up-ticket move id' that is never named anywhere in the plan; the test cannot be written or verified without knowing the specific move id string.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC4: 'Proof: `pytest tests/unit/test_plan_review.py -k coach_mapping_followup` asserts selected move id == "<exact_id_here>", not any defer-variant id.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the What section whether AC4's 'follow-up-ticket move id' refers to an existing registry entry (name it) or a third new move that must be added — so the deliverable scope is fully explicit.
findings.finding: AC4 asserts a 'follow-up-ticket move id is selected,' but no such move is introduced in the What section — only the foundation/enhancement move is described as a new addition. If the 'follow-up ticket' move does not already exist in the registry, AC4 implicitly requires a third registry change that is unspecified, making the deliverable scope ambiguous.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add a line such as: 'AC4 relies on existing move N (id: discovered_from_followup_ticket) already in the registry — no new entry needed' OR 'Add a third move with id discovered_from_followup_ticket with template … scoped to post-hoc-validation findings.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify (or add) the specific move_id that routes post-hoc-validation findings to a follow-up ticket, and reference that id explicitly in AC4's proof specification.
findings.finding: AC4 proof states 'a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected', implying a 'follow-up-ticket' move already has a known move_id. Inspection of MOVE_REGISTRY (passes.py:686-731) shows moves 1-9, 11, 12 — none is named 'follow-up ticket' or similar. The plan scope does not add this move either, so the referenced move_id is unbacked.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'Proof: a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected, not a defer.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) specify adding a `follow-up-ticket` / `discovered_from` move to plan-review's MOVE_REGISTRY with its template and `applies_when` tags, or (b) clarify that AC4's test exercises an existing move (name the move_id) that the LLM should prefer for post-hoc validation findings.
findings.finding: AC4 requires 'a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected, not a defer' for post-hoc validation, but no follow-up-ticket move exists in the plan-review MOVE_REGISTRY (passes.py:686-731) and the plan does not specify adding one. Code-review has `'file-follow-on-ticket'` (code_review/moves.py:62) but plan-review has no equivalent. The test would reference a move_id that does not exist in the registry the test exercises, making the test un-writable as specified.
findings.location: AC line 4: 'Coaching for post-hoc validation routes to a discovered_from follow-up ticket, never deferring the current AC. Proof: a coach-mapping test asserting the follow-up-ticket move id is selected, not a defer.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or fold into AC2's proof step) that explicitly verifies the updated move 9 template does not imply `in_session_evaluable` independently blocks (e.g. a string-match or comment assertion in the test).
findings.finding: The F1 phrasing note ('F1 as a whole is the blocking criterion; in_session_evaluable is its checklist item -- do not imply the sub-item independently blocks') is described as a deliverable/constraint in the What section (explicitly 'folded into AC') but no acceptance criterion verifies it was implemented correctly or that the sharpened move 9 template does not imply independent blocking.
findings.location: What section — F1 phrasing note; Acceptance Criteria (all four ACs)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC2: 'Proof: doc table matches passes.py MOVE_REGISTRY (diff check); additionally, the move 9 template must not contain language that implies in_session_evaluable is an independent blocking criterion (assert absence of such phrasing in test_plan_review.py).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Supply the full verbatim replacement template string for move 9 in the plan so the executor has no authoring decision to make.
findings.finding: Move 9's updated template string is described only conceptually ('also cover restating an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable one') without a verbatim new string; the executor must compose the design choice, which is a deferred decision.
findings.location: What section: 'Sharpen move 9 IN-PLACE' bullet; AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line such as: 'New move-9 template: "Plan how {subject} will be verified before implementing it; if the current success target is not observable in-session, restate it as one that is."'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Supply the verbatim new move 9 template string in the plan (just as the new foundation/enhancement move carries its 'verbatim locked template'), so the executor has no authoring latitude over LLM-facing coaching prose.
findings.finding: The sharpened move 9 template text is not provided. The plan says to update move 9 in-place to 'also cover restating an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable one' and notes 'Back-out = revert the template string' — implying the executor must produce a new template. But no new template string is given anywhere in the plan. The executor must author new LLM-facing prose, which contradicts the deterministic-template principle and is exactly the kind of open-ended authoring the locked-template design prevents.
findings.location: What section: 'Sharpen move 9 IN-PLACE … from "Plan how {subject} will be verified before implementing it." to also cover restating an unobservable/deferred success target as an in-session-observable one.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'New move 9 template: "Plan how {subject} will be verified in-session — restate any post-sprint-only or deferred success target as a deterministic command or repo-artifact check."' (or author equivalent locked text).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test assertion (or extend AC1) that verifies the foundation/enhancement move is suppressed when the finding's tags do NOT include a sizing/coverage tag, exercising the applies_when gate in both directions.
findings.finding: The applies_when gate on the new foundation/enhancement move — which restricts it to sizing/coverage findings — has no negative-path test: the plan specifies no test asserting the move is NOT offered for non-sizing/coverage finding types.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1; What section (applies_when field)
findings.suggested_fix: In AC1, add: 'and a negative-path assertion that the foundation/enhancement move is absent from offered moves when the finding carries a non-sizing/non-coverage tag (e.g., correctness or security).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign an explicit string id (e.g. `'10'`) to the new foundation/enhancement move in the What section, and clarify whether AC4's 'follow-up-ticket move id' refers to this same move or a second new move that must also be added.
findings.finding: The plan never assigns a move id to the new foundation/enhancement move. AC1 and AC4 reference it without naming an id, yet the MOVE_REGISTRY is keyed by string id (e.g. '1'–'12') and the LLM must emit the id to select the move.
findings.location: What section ('Add foundation/enhancement move'); AC1; AC4

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify and test load_move_registry merge semantics (base-wins, extension-wins, or error-on-collision) for the new foundation/enhancement move and the updated move 9 template, and add at least one AC covering the merged-registry path.
findings.finding: The plan mutates MOVE_REGISTRY (adds a new move, updates move 9 in-place) but does not specify the merge/collision behavior in load_move_registry when a project extension defines a key that conflicts with the new foundation/enhancement move or the revised move 9 template. If a project extension wins on collision, the new move may be silently suppressed or move 9's sharpened template overwritten; if the base wins, project customizations are lost.
findings.location: What section — 'load_move_registry (passes.py:736) merges project extensions'; AC line 1 (foundation/enhancement move added)
findings.suggested_fix: In the AC, add: 'load_move_registry with a conflicting project extension either (a) raises a KeyError/warning on collision so the operator is alerted, or (b) applies a documented precedence rule; add a pytest parametrized case covering each path.' Decide and document the rule in a comment adjacent to load_move_registry.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief 'locked vs. open template' authoring note to docs/plan-review-gate.md (or CLAUDE.md) explaining when a move should restrict LLM substitution to only {subject}, and include its verification in AC2's diff/doc-check scope.
findings.finding: The 'locked template' convention (LLM fills only {subject}, no other slots) is a novel authoring constraint for MOVE_REGISTRY entries but the plan's doc update scope is limited to keeping the move table diff-identical — it does not capture the locked-template rule as a documented convention in docs/plan-review-gate.md or CLAUDE.md, leaving future move authors without guidance.
findings.location: What section — 'Add foundation/enhancement move with a LOCKED template'; AC bullet 2 — 'doc table matches passes.py MOVE_REGISTRY (diff check)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the docs/plan-review-gate.md update, add a one-paragraph 'Move authoring conventions' section noting: 'Moves intended as prescriptive coaching language use a locked template — only {subject} is substitutable; the LLM must not paraphrase the rest. Use an open template only when the coaching language itself should be situation-adapted.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the back-out statement for move 9 to explicitly include reverting docs/plan-review-gate.md in lockstep: 'Back-out = revert the template string in passes.py AND revert the corresponding row in docs/plan-review-gate.md.'
findings.finding: The back-out / rollback path for the move 9 in-place edit covers only passes.py ('Back-out = revert the template string') but AC2 requires docs/plan-review-gate.md to be updated in lockstep with passes.py. The rollback path does not mention reverting the docs, leaving the rollback incomplete for a stated required synchronized change.
findings.location: What section — move 9 paragraph ('Back-out = revert the template string'); AC2 ('docs/plan-review-gate.md move table updated identically')
findings.suggested_fix: Change the back-out note to: 'Back-out = revert the template string in passes.py:686 AND revert the corresponding move 9 row in docs/plan-review-gate.md (lines ~148-160) to the prior text.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
